title: Tamil Sambar

subs.name: moong dal for a lighter, faster sambar — reduce pressure to 3 whistles

ingredients.name: drumstick pods, cut in 4 cm lengths
visual_checks: or 200 g ash gourd or potato
ingredients.notes: or 200 g ash gourd or potato

subs.name: 200 g ash gourd or 2 medium potatoes, cubed
subs.notes: add potato 8 minutes before it would be done

ingredients.name: tamarind
visual_checks: soak in 150 ml warm water, squeeze out pulp
ingredients.notes: soak in 150 ml warm water, squeeze out pulp

subs.name: 2 tsp coriander powder + 1 tsp red chilli powder + ½ tsp cumin

steps.text: Rinse the toor dal twice and pressure cook with 2 cups of water and the turmeric for 4 whistles on medium flame. Let pressure release naturally, then mash the dal to a smooth porridge with the back of a ladle.
Soak the tamarind in 150 ml warm water for 10 minutes. Squeeze out all the pulp through your fingers and discard the fibres. Set the extract aside.

Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a kadai over medium flame. Add the shallots and cook until they soften and turn golden at the edges, about 4 minutes. Add the tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook until they collapse.

Pour in the tamarind extract and 300 ml water. Add the sambar powder, salt, and drumstick pieces. Bring to a boil, then simmer on medium for 12 minutes until the drumstick is fully tender and the raw tamarind smell has cooked off.
Add the mashed dal and stir well. If the sambar is too thick, add up to 100 ml hot water. Simmer 5 more minutes so the dal absorbs the tamarind-spice base.

In a small pan, heat the remaining tablespoon of oil. Splutter mustard seeds, add urad dal and fry until golden, then add dried red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida. Fry 30 seconds until the leaves crisp.
Pour the tempering over the sambar and stir in the coriander leaves. Check salt and remove from heat. Rest 5 minutes before serving — the sambar thickens slightly as it sits and the flavours deepen.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: drumstick → 200 g ash gourd or 2 medium potatoes, cubed (add potato 8 minutes before it would be done); toor-dal → moong dal for a lighter, faster sambar — reduce pressure to 3 whistles; sambar-powder → 2 tsp coriander powder + 1 tsp red chilli powder + ½ tsp cumin; shallot → 1 medium red onion, sliced
